cycle_label: GOV-08 — governance-thread standalone sprint · fix denormalized deployment_name staleness on reconciliation suggestions (reader fix + backfill) · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-08 = 8). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-08 CLOSED 2026-05-15, same-day open-to-close, one loop clean. Scoped from a GOV-07 carryforward investigation: the nodemad deployment surfaced as TWO buckets in the reconciliation suggestion grouping ('nodemad' + 'nodemad — N0D3MAD-01 living portable DArnTech platform') but all 5 suggestions shared deployment_id c6fa7aa0, and the deployment's current name was 'nodemad — NODEMAD-03 voice-capture-appliance + fleet-template'. The 'duplicate' was a denormalization staleness defect: casey-junior's reconciler snapshots deployment_name into each suggestion at creation time (reconciler.py:329) and never re-attributes when the deployment is renamed. Same structural shape as the GOV-06 L04 append-only-ingester-stale-mapping finding. Inventory at L01 open confirmed the bug was suggestions-only — log_reconciliation_activity stores only deployment_id (clean by design); other deployment_name usages read from the live deployments dict. GOV-08 L01 landed: (1) reader-side resolution via get_current_deployment_names() at GET /suggestions + GET /suggestions/summary so the dashboard stops hallucinating phantom buckets immediately; (2) idempotent backfill POST /api/reconciliation/refresh-deployment-names (fill-only) for storage. Deployed casey-junior, prod-verified the phantom bucket collapsed to a single c6fa7aa0 entry with the current name, ran the backfill (7 refreshed / 80 unchanged / 0 orphans), confirmed idempotency (second pass = 0 to refresh). Codified the generalized denormalization-staleness-pattern memory (n=3 now: L04 git_events, GOV-08 suggestions, plus the activity log as the clean counter-example showing what the right shape looks like). GOV-09 opens from a fresh sweep when next scheduled.
goals: L01 — reader fix + backfill + close. DONE. casey-junior commit 0660bb4 added get_current_deployment_names() helper + refresh_deployment_names() backfill in app/services/reconciler.py, patched GET /api/reconciliation/suggestions + GET /api/reconciliation/suggestions/summary to resolve names from the live deployments dict at read time, and added POST /api/reconciliation/refresh-deployment-names endpoint mirroring L04's remap shape. `make deploy` clean. Prod-verified: all 5 nodemad-c6fa7aa0 suggestions now return current name 'nodemad — NODEMAD-03 voice-capture-appliance + fleet-template' on /suggestions; /suggestions/summary collapsed the phantom bucket to one entry. Ran the backfill on prod: 7 refreshed (6 from 'nodemad', 1 from 'nodemad — N0D3MAD-01 living portable DArnTech platform'), 80 unchanged, 0 orphans. Second dry-run verified idempotency (0 to refresh). Codified memory/denormalization-staleness-pattern.md generalizing the n=3 instances + the activity-log counter-example. GOV-08 closed.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-08 is CLOSED — single loop clean, no carryover, no escalations. Phantom nodemad bucket eliminated on prod; storage backfilled; generalized denormalization-staleness pattern codified to memory (n=3 + counter-example). To open GOV-09: run a fresh health-check sweep FIRST per the operating model. Standing instruments remain the GOV-06 set: doc-health-check.timer (08:15) and observatory-doc-health-snapshot.timer (23:50). Known carryforward concerns for the next sweep: (1) the reconciler-confidence finding (memory/reconciler-suggestion-confidence-deployment-scoped.md) still uncommitted to a structural fix — operator review remains the only guard against deployment-scoped false positives; (2) the 22 still-pending reconciliation suggestions across 8 deployments — operator-review work, not GOV-shaped; (3) deferred from GOV-07 close — scan other dashboard action surfaces for any discredited-mechanism prescriptions (the GOV-07 carry-audit found none); (4) NEW — the denormalization-staleness-pattern memory's how-to-apply suggests greping any new repo or fresh codebase for `*_name` columns sitting next to `*_id` columns of the same entity, as a periodic hygiene check.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-08 opened 2026-05-15 during a GOV-07 carryforward investigation. The 'duplicate nodemad deployment' flagged in the GOV-07 post-close addendum turned out to be a denormalization staleness defect: same structural pattern as GOV-06 L04's append-only-ingester-stale-mapping. Operator ratified the recommended scope: reader-side fix + backfill (both, the most thorough option).
stakes: The /ops dashboard's reconciliation grouping shows phantom deployments whenever a deployment has been renamed since its suggestions were created. Currently visible: 'nodemad' + 'nodemad — N0D3MAD-01 living portable DArnTech platform' both map to deployment_id c6fa7aa0, whose current name is 'nodemad — NODEMAD-03 voice-capture-appliance + fleet-template' — neither bucket name is right. The defect's reach is wider than the single visible symptom: every rename creates a permanent stale entry, and operators reviewing suggestions can't trust which deployment they're triaging. The fix has two parts because each addresses a different failure window: reader-side resolution gives correct display going forward even on stale storage; the backfill pass cleans existing storage so audits, exports, and any non-reader consumer also see truth. Same shape as L04 and the family captured in append-only-ingester-stale-mapping — this loop generalizes the finding into a pattern (denormalization-staleness) and proves the discipline by addressing both the read and write surfaces in the same sprint.
notes: GOV-08 opened 2026-05-15. Inventory completed at open (L01 first half): denormalized deployment_name lives ONLY in suggestions array of reconciliation.json (reconciler.py:329 snapshots it at creation). log_reconciliation_activity stores only deployment_id (clean by design — when the activity reader needs a name, it joins live). Reader-fix surfaces: GET /api/reconciliation/suggestions (line 39) and GET /api/reconciliation/suggestions/summary (line 63). Backfill mirrors the L04 remap_git_events shape (idempotent, fill-only). Related memory: append-only-ingester-stale-mapping (the precedent, n=2 → n=3 with this loop); fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation (the analogous discipline for action hints); cascade-rc-rename-consumer-runtime-gap (the original family). Casey Junior deployment 09c22323. Memory directory now repo-tracked at governance-thread/memory/ via symlink — this sprint's durable findings land in git history.
